content:
I am a Mechanical Engineer specialized in the scale-up of electrochemical systems, with a focus on integrated CO2 capture and conversion. I’m passionate about bringing lab-scale innovations into the real world, scaling electrochemical climate tech for industrial deployment and a sustainable future.Resume & CV#Download Resume 1-page professional overview
Download CV comprehensive academic record
Innovation & Leadership#Program Lead2023 – 2025SintonLab, University of Toronto, Toronto, ONManaged a multi-year research partnership with Shell, setting the strategic technical direction for energy-efficient, stable, and scalable integrated CO2 capture and conversion electrolyzers.Co-Founder2021 – 2022E-quester, Toronto, ONCo-founded an electrochemical direct air capture start-up and led the team to win a $250K Student Prize in the XPRIZE Carbon Removal competition, managing business development and operational strategy.Hardware & Scale-up#Electrolyzer Fabrication Coordinator2023 – 2025SintonLab, University of Toronto, Toronto, ONOverhauled machining workflows and standardized SolidWorks models to serve as the primary technical liaison for complex electrolyzer manufacturing.Doctoral Researcher2024CSIRO Energy Center, Newcastle, NSWDeveloped and scaled CO2 electrolyzers from 1 cm2 to 100 cm2 for integrated capture and conversion systems.Pilot Operator2020CERT Systems Inc., Calgary, ABOperated a 40,000 cm2 pilot plant converting CO2 into ethylene as a Top 10 Finalist in the NRG COSIA Carbon XPRIZE.Mechanical Design Intern2017Modular Construction, University of Alberta, Edmonton, ABDesigned and modeled an industrial multi-function CNC machine to automate fastening and cutting for wooden wall production.Industry & Infrastructure#Energy Analysis Intern2020–2021University of Calgary, Calgary, ABAnalyzed energy performance and thermal data for the MacKimmie Tower, a net-zero carbon building, delivering actionable recommendations to improve operational efficiency.Project Engineering Intern2019WSP in Canada, Calgary, ABDeveloped a proprietary Class V capital cost estimation tool for industrial facilities and transformed legacy P&IDs into a 3D-linked compliance tool for methane regulations.Project Management Intern2018City of Edmonton, Edmonton, ABLed public engagement for major infrastructure, securing buy-in from 250+ stakeholders and city officials while authorizing change orders to maintain project milestones.Equipment Reliability Engineering Intern2017Syncrude Canada Ltd., Fort McMurray, ABEngineered and delivered reliability packages to resolve emergency equipment failures, ensuring full compliance with ASME and API codes.Education#Ph.D. Mechanical Engineering2025University of Toronto, Toronto, ONThesis: Electrochemical systems for reactive capture of CO2Supervisor: Prof. David SintonB.A.Sc. Mechanical Engineering2020University of Alberta, Edmonton, ABBeyond the Stack#Recently, I’ve also been occupied with:
Building cardio endurance as a beginner runner, guided by an AI trainer who constantly tells me to drop my ego and slow down, because how else do you make small talk in the Bay Area these days? 👟 Rotating my Monstera around the house to find the optimal sunlight and maximize fenestrations. 🌿 Tweaking traditional East and South Asian recipes to incorporate more fiber and protein without compromising authenitc flavours. Well, some compromises, because fat is flavour, but I don’t want my doctor to get mad at me. 🍳 I’d love to hear from you if you’d like to chat about scaling electrochemical climate tech, have suggestions for Ionic Stacks, or have questions about the PhD experience.
Say hello 📩
title: About

content: Short blurb test for CDR landing page.
title: Carbon Dioxide Removal (CDR)

content:
The air around us contains an average of 425 ppm (0.0425 vol%) of CO₂. While this may not sound like much, it is precisely why DAC represents one of the greatest engineering challenges in climate tech: it is an absolute uphill battle against thermodynamics. While conventional carbon capture relies on heat for the regeneration process, electrochemistry can drive the separation using electrons, allowing the system to operate entirely on a renewable grid.
pH-Swing Architecture#When CO₂ (an acid) is captured by a highly alkaline solvent (a base) in an air contactor, it transforms into an ionic form, generating a CO₂-rich solution. The electrochemical stack then drives the cycle by shifting the pH down to trigger the release of concentrated CO₂, then shifting it back up to regenerate the solvent. This returns a CO₂-lean solution to the air contactor and completes the loop.
flowchart LR%% define the nodesAmbient[/"Ambient Air"/]Contactor["Air Contactor"]Stack[["Stack"]]Pure[/"High-Purity CO₂"/]%% define the flowAmbient --> ContactorContactor -->|CO₂-Rich| StackStack --> |CO₂-Lean| ContactorStack --> Pure%% stylingclassDef StackStyle padding:25px 50px,stroke-width:4px,font-weight:bold,font-size:18px;class Stack StackStyleElectro-Swing Architecture#These electrodes are like “switchable CO₂ magnets.” Rather than circulating liquid electrolytes, this architecture relies on redox-active electrodes that change their chemical affinity based on the applied voltage. The system captures CO₂ while in a reduced state and releases it in an oxidized state when the polarity is reversed.
flowchart LR%% define the nodesAmbient[/"Ambient Air"/]Stack[["Stack"]]Pure[/"High-Purity CO₂"/]%% define the flowAmbient --> StackStack --> Pure%% stylingclassDef StackStyle padding:25px 50px,stroke-width:4px,font-weight:bold,font-size:18px;class Stack StackStyle
title: Direct Air Capture (DAC)
